question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFileSignature object where getNode().getPublicKey() returns null, causing verifySignature() to silently return false and skip cryptographic verification, so that a tampered transaction stream file is never flagged as invalid and proceeds to consensusValidator.validate() with DOWNLOADED status?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileSignature with a null fileHashSignature field, causing verifySignature() to log an error and return false without throwing, so that a tampered transaction file's signature is silently dropped from the VERIFIED set and the tampered file may still reach consensus if enough other nodes are compromised?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger any Exception inside the try block of verifySignature() (e.g., by providing malformed fileHash bytes that cause Signature.update() to throw), causing the catch block to silently return false, so that the signature is treated as unverified rather than raising an alert, allowing a tampered transaction stream to avoid detection?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a StreamFileSignature where metadataHashSignature is null, causing verifySignature() to skip the metadata hash verification block entirely and return true after only verifying fileHash, so that a tampered metadata portion of a transaction stream file is accepted as fully verified?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Signature object state reuse between fileHash and metadataHash verification — where sig.update(fileHash) is called, sig.verify() is called, then sig.update(metadataHash) is called on the same Signature instance — to cause incorrect cryptographic state and accept a tampered metadataHash as valid?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an empty Collection<StreamFileSignature> to verify(), causing the for-loop to execute zero iterations and consensusValidator.validate() to be called with an empty collection, potentially bypassing all signature checks and allowing tampered transaction data to be accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature whose status is pre-set to VERIFIED (SignatureStatus.VERIFIED) before verify() is called, causing the loop to skip verifySignature() for that entry and directly pass it to consensusValidator.validate() as already verified, so that a tampered transaction file's signature is never cryptographically checked?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a SignatureType whose getAlgorithm() returns a weak or attacker-controlled algorithm string (e.g., 'MD5withRSA'), causing Signature.getInstance() to instantiate a weak algorithm, so that a forged signature over tampered transaction data passes cryptographic verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a SignatureType whose getProvider() returns a malicious or attacker-controlled JCA provider name, causing Signature.getInstance() to load an untrusted provider that always returns true for sig.verify(), so that a tampered transaction stream file's signature is accepted as valid?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a fileHash byte array of zero length or all-zero bytes, causing sig.update() to process an empty/trivial hash, so that sig.verify() against a pre-computed signature over empty bytes succeeds and a tampered transaction file is accepted as verified?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a fileHashSignature byte array that is a valid RSA signature over an empty or trivial message, then pair it with a matching trivial fileHash, causing sig.verify() to return true and the tampered transaction stream file to be marked VERIFIED?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user cause consensusValidator.validate() to receive a collection where all StreamFileSignature entries have status DOWNLOADED (none VERIFIED), so that the signatureHashMap in ConsensusValidatorImpl is empty, and if consensusRatio is ZERO the validation is bypassed, allowing tampered transaction data to be accepted without any verified signatures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileSignature where getNode() returns a ConsensusNode with getPublicKey() returning a public key that does not correspond to any real network node, causing sig.initVerify() to succeed but sig.verify() to return false, so that the signature is silently rejected and the tampered file's detection depends solely on whether enough other nodes' signatures are present?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a StreamFileSignature with a metadataHash of null while metadataHashSignature is non-null, causing sig.update(null) to throw a NullPointerException that is caught by the catch(Exception e) block, returning false and silently discarding the signature, so that a tampered metadata stream is never flagged?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the order of StreamFileSignature entries in the Collection passed to verify(), so that signatures from nodes with higher stake are processed last and their VERIFIED status is set too late to influence the consensus calculation in ConsensusValidatorImpl, causing a tampered file to fail consensus even though enough stake exists?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verifySignature() catches all Exception types (not just cryptographic ones), so that a StackOverflowError or OutOfMemoryError during signature verification is silently swallowed and returns false, causing the system to silently drop valid signatures and potentially fail to reach consensus on a legitimate transaction stream?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileSignature with a fileHashSignature that is an empty byte array (length 0), causing sig.verify(new byte[0]) to throw a SignatureException that is caught and returns false, so that the signature is silently discarded rather than raising an alert about a potentially tampered transaction file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user inject duplicate StreamFileSignature entries (same node, same filename) into the Collection passed to verify(), causing verifySignature() to be called multiple times for the same signature, and if the first call sets status to VERIFIED, the duplicate entry also gets VERIFIED, inflating the apparent consensus count in ConsensusValidatorImpl and allowing a tampered file to reach consensus with fewer real nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a StreamFileSignature where getSignatureType() returns null, causing a NullPointerException when calling streamFileSignature.getSignatureType().getAlgorithm(), which is caught by the catch block and returns false, so that the signature is silently dropped and a tampered transaction file avoids detection?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFileSignature where fileHash is a valid hash of a legitimate transaction file but fileHashSignature is a valid signature over a different (tampered) hash, causing sig.verify() to return false, but if the attacker controls enough node entries to make all signatures fail, the system throws SignatureVerificationException rather than accepting the tampered file — is there a race condition or retry path that could be exploited?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Collection<StreamFileSignature> passed to verify() to be modified concurrently (e.g., via a shared mutable collection) while the for-loop iterates, causing a ConcurrentModificationException that propagates out of verify() uncaught, disrupting the signature verification pipeline and potentially allowing tampered transaction files to be processed without verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileSignature where getNode() throws a RuntimeException (e.g., lazy-loaded entity throws), causing the exception to propagate out of verifySignature() uncaught (since the try block only wraps the crypto operations), disrupting the entire verify() loop and preventing legitimate signatures from being verified?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a StreamFileSignature where getFileHash() returns a byte array that is longer than the expected SHA-384 hash length (48 bytes), causing sig.update() to process extra attacker-controlled bytes, potentially allowing a signature forgery attack where the attacker pre-computes a valid signature over the padded input?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that after sig.verify(fileHashSignature) is called, the Signature object's internal state is reset, and then sig.update(metadataHash) is called without re-initializing via sig.initVerify(), potentially causing the JCA provider to behave unexpectedly and accept an invalid metadataHashSignature over tampered metadata?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where all StreamFileSignature entries in the collection have their node's getPublicKey() return null, causing all verifySignature() calls to return false, so that consensusValidator.validate() receives a collection with no VERIFIED entries, and if the consensusRatio is ZERO, validation passes and tampered transaction data is accepted?
